tEn: COMET: Commonsense Transformers for Automatic Knowledge Graph Construction
tZh: COMET：用于自动知识图谱构建的常识变换器
sEn: Generative models can automatically construct commonsense knowledge graphs with near-human precision.
sZh: 生成模型能够以接近人类的精度自动构建常识知识图谱。
absEn: We present the first comprehensive study on automatic knowledge base construction for two prevalent commonsense knowledge graphs: ATOMIC (Sap et al., 2019) and ConceptNet (Speer et al., 2017). Contrary to many conventional KBs that store knowledge with canonical templates, commonsense KBs only store loosely structured open-text descriptions of knowledge. We posit that an important step toward automatic commonsense completion is the development of generative models of commonsense knowledge, and propose COMmonsEnse Transformers (COMET) that learn to generate rich and diverse commonsense descriptions in natural language. Despite the challenges of commonsense modeling, our investigation reveals promising results when implicit knowledge from deep pre-trained language models is transferred to generate explicit knowledge in commonsense knowledge graphs. Empirical results demonstrate that COMET is able to generate novel knowledge that humans rate as high quality, with up to 77.5% (ATOMIC) and 91.7% (ConceptNet) precision at top 1, which approaches human performance for these resources. Our findings suggest that using generative commonsense models for automatic commonsense KB completion could soon be a plausible alternative to extractive methods.
absZh: 我们首次对两个流行的常识知识图谱（ATOMIC和ConceptNet）的自动知识库构建进行了全面研究。与许多使用规范模板存储知识的传统知识库不同，常识知识库只存储松散结构的开放文本描述。我们认为，自动常识补全的一个重要步骤是开发常识知识的生成模型，并提出了COMmonsEnse Transformers（COMET），该模型学习生成丰富多样的自然语言常识描述。尽管常识建模面临挑战，但我们的研究表明，当深层预训练语言模型中的隐式知识被迁移以生成常识知识图谱中的显式知识时，结果令人鼓舞。实验结果表明，COMET能够生成人类评价为高质量的新知识，在top-1精度上分别达到77.5%（ATOMIC）和91.7%（ConceptNet），接近人类在这些资源上的表现。我们的发现表明，使用生成式常识模型进行自动常识知识库补全可能很快成为抽取式方法的可行替代方案。

contrib.en: Proposes COMET, a generative model for commonsense knowledge base construction using pre-trained transformers.
contrib.zh: 提出COMET，一种利用预训练Transformer生成常识知识库的生成式模型。

contrib.en: Achieves 77.5% and 91.7% top-1 precision on ATOMIC and ConceptNet, approaching human performance.
contrib.zh: 在ATOMIC和ConceptNet上分别达到77.5%和91.7%的top-1准确率，接近人类水平。

contrib.en: Demonstrates transfer of implicit knowledge from pre-trained language models to explicit commonsense generation.
contrib.zh: 展示了从预训练语言模型将隐式知识迁移到显式常识生成的能力。

contrib.en: Shows high novelty and diversity in generated knowledge, with over 75% novel tuples having significant edit distance from training data.
contrib.zh: 生成的知识具有高新颖性和多样性，超过75%的新元组与训练数据编辑距离显著。

contrib.en: Provides a framework adaptable to different commonsense knowledge bases and relation types.
contrib.zh: 提供了一个可适应不同常识知识库和关系类型的框架。

contrib.en: Conducts comprehensive human evaluation and ablation studies validating model effectiveness.
contrib.zh: 进行了全面的人工评估和消融研究，验证了模型的有效性。

limits.en: Novel generations sometimes simplify training tuples, e.g., 'doctor CapableOf save life' vs 'save person life'.
limits.zh: 新生成的元组有时会简化训练元组，例如'doctor CapableOf save life' vs 'save person life'。

limits.en: Performance drops significantly with only 1% training data, indicating data dependency.
limits.zh: 仅使用1%训练数据时性能显著下降，表明对数据的依赖性。

limits.en: Model may produce incorrect commonsense due to ambiguous word senses, e.g., 'dove SymbolOf submarine'.
limits.zh: 模型可能因词义歧义产生错误常识，例如'dove SymbolOf submarine'。

limits.en: Evaluation limited to English; applicability to other languages is unexplored.
limits.zh: 评估仅限于英语，未探索其他语言的适用性。

limits.en: Requires seed knowledge tuples; cannot generate from scratch without existing KB.
limits.zh: 需要种子知识元组，无法在没有现有知识库的情况下从头生成。

items.en: We present the first comprehensive study on automatic knowledge base construction for two prevalent commonsense knowledge graphs: Atomic Sap et al. (2019) and ConceptNet Speer et al. (2017). Contrary to many conventional KBs that store knowledge with canonical templates, commonsense KBs only store loosely structured open-text descriptions of knowledge. We posit that an important step toward automatic commonsense completion is the development of generative models of commonsense knowledge, and propose COMmonsEnse Transformers (ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋) that learn to generate rich and diverse commonsense descriptions in natural language. Despite the challenges of commonsense modeling, our investigation reveals promising results when implicit knowledge from deep pre-trained language models is transferred to generate explicit knowledge in commonsense knowledge graphs. Empirical results demonstrate that ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is able to generate novel knowledge that humans rate as high quality, with up to 77.5% (Atomic) and 91.7% (ConceptNet) precision at top 1, which approaches human performance for these resources. Our findings suggest that using generative commonsense models for automatic commonsense KB completion could soon be a plausible alternative to extractive methods.
items.zh: 我们提出了首个针对两种常见常识知识图谱（Atomic Sap et al., 2019 和 ConceptNet Speer et al., 2017）的自动知识库构建综合研究。与许多使用规范模板存储知识的传统知识库不同，常识知识库仅存储松散结构的开放文本知识描述。我们认为，迈向自动常识补全的重要一步是开发常识知识的生成模型，并提出了 COMmonsEnse Transformers (ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋)，该模型学习以自然语言生成丰富多样的常识描述。尽管常识建模面临挑战，我们的研究揭示了令人鼓舞的结果：当深层预训练语言模型中的隐式知识被迁移以生成常识知识图谱中的显式知识时，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 能够生成人类评价为高质量的新颖知识，在 top-1 精度上分别达到 77.5%（Atomic）和 91.7%（ConceptNet），接近这些资源的人类表现。我们的发现表明，使用生成式常识模型进行自动常识知识库补全可能很快成为抽取式方法的可行替代方案。

items.capEn: Figure 1: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 learns from an existing knowledge base (solid lines) to be able to generate novel nodes and edges (dashed lines).
items.capZh: 图 1：ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 从现有知识库（实线）学习，能够生成新的节点和边（虚线）。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Model diagram. (a) In the multi-headed attention module, the key, value, and query all pass through a head-specific projection before a scaled dot-product attention is computed between them. The outputs of the heads are concatenated and projected. (b) Inside the transformer block, the outputs of all the previous layer blocks from earlier time steps are input to the multi-headed attention with the preceding block for the current time step as the query. (c) Each token is an input to a first-layer block along with all preceding tokens. Dotted lines indicate outputs to all future blocks in the next layer and inputs from all preceding blocks in the previous layer.
items.capZh: 图 2：模型示意图。(a) 在多头注意力模块中，键、值和查询在计算缩放点积注意力之前都经过头特定的投影。各头的输出被拼接并投影。(b) 在 Transformer 块内部，来自较早时间步的所有前一层块的输出与当前时间步的前一块作为查询一起输入到多头注意力中。(c) 每个 token 与所有前面的 token 一起作为第一层块的输入。虚线表示输出到下一层的所有未来块，以及来自前一层所有前面块的输入。

items.en: When reading text, humans make commonsense inferences that frame their understanding of the narrative being presented. For machines to achieve this capability, they must be able to acquire relevant and correct commonsense for an unbounded set of situations. In this work, we cast commonsense acquisition as knowledge base construction and investigate whether large-scale language models can effectively learn to generate the knowledge necessary to automatically construct a commonsense knowledge base (KB).
items.zh: 阅读文本时，人类会进行常识推理，从而构建对叙述的理解。为了使机器具备这种能力，它们必须能够为无限的情境获取相关且正确的常识。在这项工作中，我们将常识获取视为知识库构建，并研究大规模语言模型是否能有效学习生成自动构建常识知识库所需的知识。

items.en: Automatic KB construction is a long-standing goal of artificial intelligence research due to the difficulty of achieving high concept coverage in high-precision curated KBs Lenat (1995); Miller (1995). Previous work has developed models capable of reading and extracting semi-structured text Suchanek et al. (2007); Hoffart et al. (2013); Auer et al. (2007); Bollacker et al. (2008) and unstructured text Dong et al. (2014); Carlson et al. (2010); Nakashole et al. (2011, 2012); Niu (2012) into relational schemas that can be queried for downstream applications. A common thread of these approaches, however, is the focus on encyclopedic knowledge, which lends itself to a well-defined space of entities and relations that can be modeled.
items.zh: 自动知识库构建是人工智能研究的长期目标，因为在高精度人工策划的知识库中实现高概念覆盖率非常困难（Lenat, 1995; Miller, 1995）。先前的工作开发了能够读取和提取半结构化文本（Suchanek et al., 2007; Hoffart et al., 2013; Auer et al., 2007; Bollacker et al., 2008）和非结构化文本（Dong et al., 2014; Carlson et al., 2010; Nakashole et al., 2011, 2012; Niu, 2012）的模型，将其转化为可查询的关系模式以用于下游应用。然而，这些方法的共同点是专注于百科知识，这类知识具有定义良好的实体和关系空间，易于建模。

items.en: Commonsense knowledge, however, does not cleanly fit into a schema comparing two entities with a known relation, leading current approaches to model “entities" as natural language phrases and relations as any concept that can link them Li et al. (2016); Sap et al. (2019). OpenIE approaches display this property of open text entities and relations Etzioni et al. (2011); Fader et al. (2011); Mausam et al. (2012), but being extractive, they only capture knowledge that is explicitly mentioned in text, limiting their applicability for capturing commonsense knowledge, which is often implicit Gordon and Van Durme (2013).
items.zh: 然而，常识知识并不完全适合比较两个实体与已知关系的模式，导致当前方法将“实体”建模为自然语言短语，将关系建模为任何可以连接它们的概念（Li et al., 2016; Sap et al., 2019）。OpenIE 方法展示了这种开放文本实体和关系的特性（Etzioni et al., 2011; Fader et al., 2011; Mausam et al., 2012），但由于是抽取式的，它们只捕获文本中明确提及的知识，限制了其捕捉通常隐含的常识知识的能力（Gordon and Van Durme, 2013）。

items.en: Meanwhile, recent progress in training deep contextualized language models Peters et al. (2018); Radford et al. (2018); Devlin et al. (2018) provides an opportunity to explore beyond extractive methods as an avenue for commonsense KB construction. These large-scale language models display impressive performance when their underlying representations are tuned to solve end tasks, achieving state-of-the-art results on a variety of complex problems. In this work, we define the COMmonsEnse Transformer (ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋), which constructs commonsense KBs by using existing tuples as a seed set of knowledge on which to train. Using this seed set, a pre-trained language model learns to adapt its learned representations to knowledge generation, and produces novel tuples that are high quality.
items.zh: 与此同时，深度上下文化语言模型的最新进展（Peters et al., 2018; Radford et al., 2018; Devlin et al., 2018）为探索超越抽取式方法进行常识知识库构建提供了机会。这些大规模语言模型在调整其底层表示以解决最终任务时表现出色，在各种复杂问题上取得了最先进的结果。在这项工作中，我们定义了 COMmonsEnse Transformer (ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋)，它通过使用现有元组作为种子知识集进行训练来构建常识知识库。利用这个种子集，预训练语言模型学会调整其学习到的表示以进行知识生成，并产生高质量的新颖元组。

items.en: We summarize our contributions in this work as follows. First, we develop a generative approach to knowledge base construction. A model must learn to produce new nodes and identify edges between existing nodes by generating phrases that coherently complete an existing seed phrase and relation type111Demo is available at https://mosaickg.apps.allenai.org/. Second, we develop a framework for using large-scale transformer language models to learn to produce commonsense knowledge tuples222Code is available at https://github.com/atcbosselut/comet-commonsense. Finally, we perform an empirical study on the quality, novelty, and diversity of the commonsense knowledge produced by our approach for two domains, Atomic and ConceptNet, as well as an efficiency study on the number of seed tuples needed to learn an effective knowledge model. The results indicate that ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is able to produce high quality tuples as human judges find that 77.5% of generated tuples for Atomic events and 91.7% of generated tuples for ConceptNet relations are correct.
items.zh: 我们总结本工作的贡献如下。首先，我们开发了一种生成式知识库构建方法。模型必须通过学习生成连贯补全现有种子短语和关系类型的短语，来产生新节点并识别现有节点之间的边。其次，我们开发了一个使用大规模 Transformer 语言模型学习生成常识知识元组的框架。最后，我们对我们的方法在两个领域（Atomic 和 ConceptNet）中产生的常识知识的质量、新颖性和多样性进行了实证研究，并对学习有效知识模型所需的种子元组数量进行了效率研究。结果表明，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 能够生成高质量元组，人类评估者发现 77.5% 的 Atomic 事件生成元组和 91.7% 的 ConceptNet 关系生成元组是正确的。

items.en: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is an adaptation framework for constructing commonsense knowledge bases from language models by training the language model on a seed set of knowledge tuples. These tuples provide ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 with the KB structure and relations that must be learned, and ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 learns to adapt the language model representations learned from pre-training to add novel nodes and edges to the seed knowledge graph.
items.zh: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 是一个通过训练语言模型在种子知识元组集上从语言模型构建常识知识库的适应框架。这些元组为 ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 提供了必须学习的知识库结构和关系，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 学习调整从预训练中学到的语言模型表示，以向种子知识图谱添加新的节点和边。

items.en: More specifically, the problem assumes ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is given a training knowledge base of natural language tuples in {s,r,o}𝑠𝑟𝑜\{s,r,o\} format, where s𝑠s is the phrase subject of the tuple, r𝑟r is the relation of the tuple, and o𝑜o is the phrase object of the tuple. For example, a ConceptNet tuple relating to “taking a nap" would be: (s𝑠s=“take a nap", r𝑟r=Causes, o𝑜o=“have energy"). The task is to generate o𝑜o given s𝑠s and r𝑟r as inputs.
items.zh: 更具体地说，该问题假设 ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 被给予一个训练知识库，其中包含自然语言元组，格式为 {s,r,o}，其中 s 是元组的主语短语，r 是元组的关系，o 是元组的宾语短语。例如，一个与“小睡”相关的 ConceptNet 元组可能是：(s=“take a nap”, r=Causes, o=“have energy”)。任务是给定 s 和 r 作为输入，生成 o。

items.en: We define Xs={x0s,…,x|s|s}superscript𝑋𝑠subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑠0…subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑠𝑠X^{s}=\{x^{s}_{0},...,x^{s}_{|s|}\} as the tokens that make up the subject of the relation, Xr={x0r,…,x|r|r}superscript𝑋𝑟subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑟0…subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑟𝑟X^{r}=\{x^{r}_{0},...,x^{r}_{|r|}\} as the tokens that make up the relation of the tuple, and Xo={x0o,…,x|o|o}superscript𝑋𝑜subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑜0…subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑜𝑜X^{o}=\{x^{o}_{0},...,x^{o}_{|o|}\} as the tokens that make up the object of the tuple. The embedding for any word x𝑥x is denoted as e𝑒e.
items.zh: 我们将 Xs={x0s,…,x|s|s} 定义为构成关系主语的 token，Xr={x0r,…,x|r|r} 定义为构成关系 token，Xo={x0o,…,x|o|o} 定义为构成关系宾语的 token。任何词 x 的嵌入表示为 e。

items.en: While ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is agnostic to the language model with which it is initialized, in this work, we use the transformer language model architecture introduced in Radford et al. (2018) (GPT), which uses multiple transformer blocks of multi-headed scaled dot product attention and fully connected layers to encode input text Vaswani et al. (2017). Figure 2 depicts different components of the GPT architecture and we define each component in more depth below.
items.zh: 虽然 ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 对初始化的语言模型是无关的，但在这项工作中，我们使用 Radford et al. (2018) 引入的 Transformer 语言模型架构（GPT），该架构使用多个 Transformer 块，每个块包含多头缩放点积注意力和全连接层来编码输入文本（Vaswani et al., 2017）。图 2 描绘了 GPT 架构的不同组件，我们在下面更深入地定义每个组件。

items.en: As shown in Figure 2(b), each transformer layer l𝑙l contains an architecturally identical transformer block (though with unique trainable parameters) that applies the following transformations to the input to the block:
items.zh: 如图 2(b) 所示，每个 Transformer 层 l 包含一个架构相同的 Transformer 块（尽管具有独特的可训练参数），该块对块的输入应用以下变换：

items.en: where MultiAttn is a multi-headed self-attention mechanism (defined below), FFN is a two-layer feed-forward network, and LayerNorm represents a layer normalization Ba et al. (2016) operation that is applied to the output of the self-attention and the feedforward network. Note that the inputs to the LayerNorm operations contain a residual connection that sums the output of and input to the previous operation.
items.zh: 其中 MultiAttn 是多头自注意力机制（定义如下），FFN 是两层前馈网络，LayerNorm 表示层归一化操作（Ba et al., 2016），应用于自注意力和前馈网络的输出。注意，LayerNorm 操作的输入包含一个残差连接，该连接将前一个操作的输出和输入相加。

items.en: The multi-headed attention module of each transformer block, shown in Figure 2(a), is identical to the one originally defined by Vaswani et al. (2017). The attention function receives three inputs, a query Q𝑄Q, key K𝐾K, and value V𝑉V. The attention is made of multiple h​e​a​d​sℎ𝑒𝑎𝑑𝑠heads that each compute a unique scaled dot product attention distribution over V𝑉V using Q𝑄Q and K𝐾K:
items.zh: 每个 Transformer 块的多头注意力模块，如图 2(a) 所示，与 Vaswani et al. (2017) 最初定义的相同。注意力函数接收三个输入：查询 Q、键 K 和值 V。注意力由多个 heads 组成，每个 head 使用 Q 和 K 计算 V 上的唯一缩放点积注意力分布：

items.en: where dksubscript𝑑𝑘d_{k} is the dimensionality of the input vectors representing the query, key and value. For each of the heads, Q𝑄Q, K𝐾K, and V𝑉V are uniquely projected prior to the attention being computed:
items.zh: 其中 dk 是表示查询、键和值的输入向量的维度。对于每个 head，Q、K 和 V 在计算注意力之前被唯一投影：

items.en: where Hisubscript𝐻𝑖H_{i} is the output of a single attention head and WiQsubscriptsuperscript𝑊𝑄𝑖W^{Q}_{i}, WiKsubscriptsuperscript𝑊𝐾𝑖W^{K}_{i}, and WiVsubscriptsuperscript𝑊𝑉𝑖W^{V}_{i} are head-specific projections for Q𝑄Q, K𝐾K, and V𝑉V, respectively. The outputs of the attention heads Hisubscript𝐻𝑖H_{i} are then concatenated:
items.zh: 其中 Hi 是单个注意力头的输出，WiQ、WiK 和 WiV 分别是 Q、K 和 V 的头特定投影。然后注意力头 Hi 的输出被拼接：

items.en: where WOsuperscript𝑊𝑂W^{O} is an output projection of the concatenated outputs of the attention heads. As shown in Figure 2(c), we follow Radford et al. (2018) and use the output of the previous layer’s transformer block as the query input for the multi-headed attention of the next block. The keys and values are outputs of the previous layer’s block for all preceding time steps:
items.zh: 其中 WO 是注意力头拼接输出的输出投影。如图 2(c) 所示，我们遵循 Radford et al. (2018)，使用前一层 Transformer 块的输出作为下一块多头注意力的查询输入。键和值是前一层块在所有先前时间步的输出：

items.en: where 𝐡tl−1={hl−1}<tsubscriptsuperscript𝐡𝑙1𝑡subscriptsuperscriptℎ𝑙1absent𝑡\mathbf{h}^{l-1}_{t}=\{h^{l-1}\}_{<t} is the set of previous layer transformer block outputs for time steps preceding t𝑡t.
items.zh: 其中 htl−1={hl−1}<t 是前一层 Transformer 块在时间步 t 之前的输出集合。

items.en: As input to the model, we represent a knowledge tuple {s,r,o}𝑠𝑟𝑜\{s,r,o\} as a concatenated sequence of the words of each item of the tuple:
items.zh: 作为模型的输入，我们将知识元组 {s,r,o} 表示为元组中每个项目的单词的拼接序列：

items.en: Since the transformer (a self-attention model) has no concept of ordering of tokens, a position embedding ptsubscript𝑝𝑡p_{t} is initialized for each absolute position in the sequence (Vaswani et al., 2017). For any input word xt∈𝐗subscript𝑥𝑡𝐗x_{t}\in\mathbf{X}, our encoding of the input is the sum of its word embedding, etsubscript𝑒𝑡e_{t} with a position embedding encoding its absolute position in the sequence 𝐗𝐗\mathbf{X}:
items.zh: 由于Transformer（一种自注意力模型）没有词序的概念，因此为序列中的每个绝对位置初始化一个位置嵌入p_t（Vaswani等人，2017）。对于任何输入词x_t ∈ X，我们对输入的编码是其词嵌入e_t与编码其在序列X中绝对位置的位置嵌入之和：

items.en: where ptsubscript𝑝𝑡p_{t} is the position embedding for time step t𝑡t, and h0superscriptℎ0h^{0} is the input to the first transformer layer.
items.zh: 其中p_t是时间步t的位置嵌入，h^0是第一个Transformer层的输入。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Input token setup for training configurations. For the Atomic dataset, the tokens of the subject, Xssuperscript𝑋𝑠X^{s} (e.g., PersonX goes to the mall) are followed by masking tokens, which is followed by a single relation token Xrsuperscript𝑋𝑟X^{r} (e.g., xIntent), and then the object tokens Xosuperscript𝑋𝑜X^{o} (e.g., to buy clothes). The model receives the same input for ConceptNet, except that a second set of masking tokens separate Xrsuperscript𝑋𝑟X^{r} and Xosuperscript𝑋𝑜X^{o} because Xrsuperscript𝑋𝑟X^{r} can have a variable number of tokens for ConceptNet (§5.2)
items.capZh: 图3：训练配置的输入词元设置。对于Atomic数据集，主体X^s的词元（例如，PersonX goes to the mall）后面跟着掩码词元，然后是一个关系词元X^r（例如，xIntent），最后是客体词元X^o（例如，to buy clothes）。对于ConceptNet，模型接收相同的输入，只是第二组掩码词元将X^r和X^o分开，因为对于ConceptNet，X^r可以有可变数量的词元（§5.2）。

items.en: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is trained to learn to produce the phrase object o𝑜o of a knowledge tuple given the tuple’s phrase subject s𝑠s and relation r𝑟r. More specifically, given the concatenation of the tokens of s𝑠s and r𝑟r: [Xs,Xr]superscript𝑋𝑠superscript𝑋𝑟[X^{s},X^{r}] as input, the model must learn to generate the tokens of o𝑜o: Xosuperscript𝑋𝑜X^{o} (See §2.1 for definitions of these variables).
items.zh: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋被训练来学习生成知识元组的短语客体o，给定元组的短语主体s和关系r。更具体地说，给定s和r的词元拼接：[X^s, X^r]作为输入，模型必须学习生成o的词元：X^o（这些变量的定义见§2.1）。

items.en: To achieve this goal, ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is trained to maximize the conditional loglikelihood of predicting the phrase object tokens, Xosuperscript𝑋𝑜X^{o}:
items.zh: 为了实现这一目标，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋被训练以最大化预测短语客体词元X^o的条件对数似然：

items.en: where |s|𝑠|s|, |r|𝑟|r|, and |o|𝑜|o| are the number of tokens in the subject phrase, relation, and object phrase, respectively. Figure 3 outlines how the tokens in s𝑠s, r𝑟r, and o𝑜o are organized for different training tasks.
items.zh: 其中|s|、|r|和|o|分别是主体短语、关系短语和客体短语中的词元数量。图3概述了不同训练任务中s、r和o的词元组织方式。

items.en: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 relies on a seed set of knowledge tuples from an existing KB to learn to produce commonsense knowledge. In this work, we use Atomic and ConceptNet as knowledge seed sets, but other commonsense knowledge resources could have been used as well as ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is domain-agnostic.
items.zh: ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋依赖于来自现有知识库的种子知识元组集来学习生成常识知识。在这项工作中，我们使用Atomic和ConceptNet作为知识种子集，但也可以使用其他常识知识资源，因为ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋是领域无关的。

items.en: Parameters are initialized to the final language model weights from Radford et al. (2018). Additional special tokens that are added to the vocabulary for fine tuning (e.g., relation embeddings such as oReact for Atomic and IsA for ConceptNet) are initialized by sampling from the standard normal distribution.
items.zh: 参数初始化为Radford等人（2018）的最终语言模型权重。为微调而添加到词汇表中的额外特殊词元（例如，Atomic的oReact和ConceptNet的IsA等关系嵌入）通过从标准正态分布中采样进行初始化。

items.en: Following Radford et al. (2018)’s design of the GPT model, we initialize ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 with 12 layers, 768-dimensional hidden states, and 12 attention heads. We use a dropout rate of 0.1 and use GeLU Hendrycks and Gimpel (2016) units as activation functions. During training, our batch size is 64. Other dataset-specific hyperparameters are provided in Appendix A.1.
items.zh: 遵循Radford等人（2018）对GPT模型的设计，我们初始化ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋为12层、768维隐藏状态和12个注意力头。我们使用0.1的丢弃率，并使用GeLU（Hendrycks和Gimpel，2016）单元作为激活函数。训练期间，我们的批量大小为64。其他数据集特定的超参数见附录A.1。

items.en: The Atomic dataset333https://homes.cs.washington.edu/~msap/atomic/, released by Sap et al. (2019), contains 877K tuples covering a variety of social commonsense knowledge around specific event prompts (e.g., “X goes to the store”). Specifically, Atomic distills its commonsense in nine dimensions, covering the event’s causes (e.g., “X needs to drive there”), its effects on the agent (e.g., “to get food”) and its effect on other direct (or implied) participants (e.g., “Others will be fed”). More details about Atomic can be found in Appendix D. For our experiments, Atomic events (e.g., “X goes to the store”) are phrase subjects, s𝑠s, the dimension (e.g., xIntent) is the phrase relation, r𝑟r, and the causes/effects (e.g., “to get food”) are phrase objects, o𝑜o. We use the training splits from Sap et al. (2019), resulting in 710k training, 80k development, and 87k test tuples respectively.
items.zh: Atomic数据集（https://homes.cs.washington.edu/~msap/atomic/），由Sap等人（2019）发布，包含877K个元组，涵盖了围绕特定事件提示（例如，“X goes to the store”）的各种社会常识知识。具体来说，Atomic在九个维度上提炼其常识，涵盖事件的原因（例如，“X needs to drive there”）、对主体的影响（例如，“to get food”）以及对其他直接（或隐含）参与者的影响（例如，“Others will be fed”）。关于Atomic的更多细节见附录D。在我们的实验中，Atomic事件（例如，“X goes to the store”）是短语主体s，维度（例如，xIntent）是短语关系r，原因/影响（例如，“to get food”）是短语客体o。我们使用Sap等人（2019）的训练划分，分别得到710k训练、80k开发和87k测试元组。

items.en: Following Sap et al. (2019), we evaluate our method using BLEU-2 as an automatic evaluation metric. We also report the perplexity of the model on its gold generations. The remaining automatic metrics in Table 1 measure the proportion of generated tuples and generated objects which are not in the training set. We report the proportion of all generated tuples that are novel (% N/T s​r​o𝑠𝑟𝑜sro) and that have a novel object (% N/T o𝑜o)444a new o𝑜o represents a new node in the knowledge graph. To show that these novel objects are diverse (i.e., the same novel object is not the only one being generated), we also report the number of novel objects as a function of the set of unique objects produced for all test set events (% N/U o𝑜o).
items.zh: 遵循Sap等人（2019），我们使用BLEU-2作为自动评估指标来评估我们的方法。我们还报告了模型在其黄金生成上的困惑度。表1中的其余自动指标衡量了生成的元组和生成的客体不在训练集中的比例。我们报告了所有生成元组中新颖的比例（% N/T sro）以及具有新颖客体的比例（% N/T o）——新颖o表示知识图谱中的一个新节点。为了显示这些新颖客体是多样化的（即，并非只有同一个新颖客体被生成），我们还报告了新颖客体数量占测试集所有事件产生的唯一客体集合的比例（% N/U o）。

items.en: Finally, we perform a human evaluation using workers from Amazon Mechanical Turk (AMT). Workers are asked to identify whether a model generation of Atomic commonsense adequately completes a plausible tuple of phrase subject, relation, and phrase object. Following the setup of Sap et al. (2019), we evaluate 100 randomly selected events from the test set. For each event and relation type, 10 candidates are generated using beam search and the full beam is evaluated by five different workers. Overall, n=5000 ratings are produced per relation (100 events ×\times 5 workers ×\times 10 candidates). The reported Avg in Table 2 is an average of these scores, yielding n=45000 total ratings for each model. We use Pitman’s test Noreen (1989) with 100k permutations to test for statistical significance. Because 50 different hypotheses are tested (9 relations + the total), the Holm-Bonferroni method Holm (1979) is used to correct significance thresholds. Example events from the development set and their generated phrase objects are available in Table 5.
items.zh: 最后，我们使用Amazon Mechanical Turk（AMT）的工作人员进行人工评估。工作人员被要求判断模型生成的Atomic常识是否充分完成了一个合理的短语主体、关系和短语客体元组。遵循Sap等人（2019）的设置，我们从测试集中随机选择100个事件进行评估。对于每个事件和关系类型，使用束搜索生成10个候选，并由5名不同的工作人员评估整个束。总共，每个关系产生n=5000个评分（100个事件 × 5名工作人员 × 10个候选）。表2中报告的Avg是这些评分的平均值，每个模型总共产生n=45000个评分。我们使用Pitman检验（Noreen，1989）进行100k次置换以测试统计显著性。由于测试了50个不同的假设（9个关系+总计），使用Holm-Bonferroni方法（Holm，1979）来校正显著性阈值。开发集中的示例事件及其生成的短语客体见表5。

items.en: We report the performance of our method against the models trained in Sap et al. (2019) that use LSTM sequence-to-sequence models Sutskever et al. (2014) to encode the input subject and relation and produce an output object.
items.zh: 我们报告了我们的方法与Sap等人（2019）训练的模型相比的性能，这些模型使用LSTM序列到序列模型（Sutskever等人，2014）来编码输入主体和关系并生成输出客体。

items.en: To evaluate how pre-training on a large corpus helps the model learn to produce knowledge, we train a version of ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 that is not initialized with pre-trained weights (ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 (- pretrain)). We also evaluate the data efficiency of our method by training models on different proportions of the training data. Finally, because the ultimate goal of our method is to be able to perform high-quality, diverse knowledge base construction, we explore how various decoding schemes affect the quality of candidate knowledge tuples. We present the effect of the following generation strategies: argmax greedy decoding, beam search with beam sizes, b=2, 5, 10, and top-k𝑘k sampling with k = 5, 10. For each decoding method, we conduct the human evaluation on the number of final candidates produced by each method.
items.zh: 为了评估在大语料库上预训练如何帮助模型学习生成知识，我们训练了一个未使用预训练权重初始化的ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋版本（ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋（-预训练））。我们还通过在不同比例的训练数据上训练模型来评估我们方法的数据效率。最后，由于我们方法的最终目标是能够进行高质量、多样化的知识库构建，我们探索了不同的解码方案如何影响候选知识元组的质量。我们展示了以下生成策略的效果：argmax贪心解码、束大小为b=2、5、10的束搜索，以及k=5、10的top-k采样。对于每种解码方法，我们对每种方法产生的最终候选数量进行人工评估。

items.en: The BLEU-2 results in Table 1 indicate that ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 exceeds the performance of all baselines, achieving a 51% relative improvement over the top performing model of Sap et al. (2019). More interesting, however, is the result of the human evaluation, where ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 reported a statistically significant relative Avg performance increase of 18% over the top baseline, Event2In(Volun). This performance increase is consistent, as well, with an improvement being observed across every relation type. In addition to the quality improvements, Table 1 shows that ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 produces more novel tuple objects than the baselines, as well.
items.zh: 表1中的BLEU-2结果表明，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋超过了所有基线的性能，相对于Sap等人（2019）的最佳性能模型实现了51%的相对改进。然而，更有趣的是人工评估的结果，其中ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋报告了相对于最佳基线Event2In(Volun)的统计显著的相对Avg性能提升18%。这种性能提升是一致的，并且在每种关系类型上都观察到了改进。除了质量改进，表1还显示ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋比基线产生了更多新颖的元组客体。

items.en: Significant differences were also observed between the performance of the model whose weights were initialized with the pre-trained parameters from the GPT model of Radford et al. (2018) and a model with the same architecture that was trained from random initialization. This 14% relative improvement in overall human performance confirms that the language representations learned by the GPT model are transferable to generating natural language commonsense knowledge.
items.zh: 在权重初始化为Radford等人（2018）GPT模型的预训练参数的模型与从随机初始化训练的相同架构模型之间也观察到了显著差异。整体人工性能14%的相对改进证实了GPT模型学习的语言表示可迁移到生成自然语言常识知识。

items.en: In Table 3, we show the effect of different generation policies on knowledge quality. The most interesting result is that using greedy decoding to produce knowledge tuples only results in a 10% relative performance gap compared to a human evaluation of the Atomic test set, showing that the knowledge produced by the model approaches human performance. While producing more total candidates does lower overall performance, quality assessments still hover around 55%777This number is partially low due to the many “none” references in the oEffect, oReact, oWant categories. In any set of 10 candidates, “none” can only be predicted once, which causes most candidates in the beam to be incorrect if “none” is the appropriate answer. for a beam size of 10. This result suggests that ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 could be effective with human evaluators in the loop to confirm the correctness of generated tuples.
items.zh: 在表3中，我们展示了不同生成策略对知识质量的影响。最有趣的结果是，使用贪心解码生成知识元组仅导致与Atomic测试集人工评估相比10%的相对性能差距，表明模型生成的知识接近人类表现。虽然生成更多总候选会降低整体性能，但质量评估仍然徘徊在55%左右（这个数字部分偏低是由于oEffect、oReact、oWant类别中的许多“none”引用。在任何10个候选集中，“none”只能被预测一次，如果“none”是正确答案，则束中的大多数候选都是错误的），对于束大小为10的情况。这一结果表明，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋在有人类评估者参与确认生成元组正确性的情况下可能是有效的。

items.en: Because not all domains will have large available commonsense KBs on which to train, we explore how varying the amount of training data available for learning affects the quality and novelty of the knowledge that is produced. Our results in Table 4 indicate that even with only 10% of the available training data, the model is still able to produce generations that are coherent, adequate, and novel. Using only 1% of the training data clearly diminishes the quality of the produced generations, with significantly lower observed results across both quality and novelty metrics. Interestingly, we note that training the model without pre-trained weights performs comparably to training with 10% of the seed tuples, quantifying the impact of using pre-trained language representations.
items.zh: 由于并非所有领域都有大型可用的常识知识库可供训练，我们探索了改变可用于学习的训练数据量如何影响生成知识的质量和新颖性。我们在表4中的结果表明，即使只有10%的可用训练数据，模型仍然能够生成连贯、充分且新颖的生成。仅使用1%的训练数据明显降低了生成质量，在质量和新颖性指标上观察到的结果显著较低。有趣的是，我们注意到，在没有预训练权重的情况下训练模型与使用10%种子元组训练的性能相当，这量化了使用预训练语言表示的影响。

items.en: The ConceptNet dataset888https://ttic.uchicago.edu/~kgimpel/commonsense.html, provided by Li et al. (2016), consists of tuples obtained from the Open Mind Common Sense (OMCS) entries in ConceptNet 5 Speer et al. (2017). Tuples are in the standard s​r​o𝑠𝑟𝑜sro form – (e.g., take a nap, Causes, have energy). The most confident 1200 tuples were used to create the test set, while the next 1200 tuples were used to create two development sets, which we combine in this work. The 100k version of the training set was used to train models, which contains 34 relation types.
items.zh: ConceptNet数据集（https://ttic.uchicago.edu/~kgimpel/commonsense.html），由Li等人（2016）提供，包含从ConceptNet 5（Speer等人，2017）中的Open Mind Common Sense（OMCS）条目获得的元组。元组采用标准的sro形式——（例如，take a nap, Causes, have energy）。最自信的1200个元组用于创建测试集，而接下来的1200个元组用于创建两个开发集，我们在本工作中合并了它们。使用100k版本的训练集来训练模型，其中包含34种关系类型。

items.en: We evaluate our models that generate ConceptNet relations using the following metrics. First, we report the perplexity of the gold relations in the test set (PPL). To evaluate the quality of generated knowledge, we also report the number of generated positive examples in the test set that are scored as correct by the pre-trained Bilinear AVG model developed by Li et al. (2016).999 A pre-trained model can be found at https://ttic.uchicago.edu/~kgimpel/comsense_resources/ckbc-demo.tar.gz For a given s​r​o𝑠𝑟𝑜sro tuple, this model produces a probability for whether the tuple is correct. We threshold scores at 50% probability to identify positive predictions. On the completion task originally proposed in Li et al. (2016), this model achieved 92.5% accuracy on the test set, indicating that it is a strong proxy for automatically evaluating whether a generated tuple is correct. Finally, we report the same novelty metrics as for Atomic: N/T s​r​o𝑠𝑟𝑜sro and N/T o𝑜o.
items.zh: 我们使用以下指标评估生成ConceptNet关系的模型。首先，我们报告测试集中黄金关系的困惑度（PPL）。为了评估生成知识的质量，我们还报告了测试集中被Li等人（2016）开发的预训练Bilinear AVG模型评为正确的生成正例的数量。对于给定的sro元组，该模型生成该元组是否正确的概率。我们将阈值设为50%概率以识别正预测。在Li等人（2016）最初提出的完成任务上，该模型在测试集上达到了92.5%的准确率，表明它是自动评估生成元组是否正确的强代理。最后，我们报告与Atomic相同的新颖性指标：N/T sro和N/T o。

items.en: As a baseline, we re-implement the BiLSTM model proposed by Saito et al. (2018) with minor modifications outlined in Appendix A.2. This model is trained to learn to encode knowledge in both directions: s​r→o→𝑠𝑟𝑜sr\rightarrow o and o​r→s→𝑜𝑟𝑠or\rightarrow s to help augment a knowledge base completion model. It is only evaluated on the s​r→o→𝑠𝑟𝑜sr\rightarrow o tuple generation task, however. For posterity, we also include the result from a LSTM model that is only trained on the s​r→o→𝑠𝑟𝑜sr\rightarrow o task (LSTM - s𝑠s).
items.zh: 作为基线，我们重新实现了Saito等人（2018）提出的BiLSTM模型，并进行了附录A.2中概述的微小修改。该模型被训练为学习双向编码知识：s​r→o和o​r→s，以帮助增强知识库补全模型。然而，它仅在s​r→o元组生成任务上进行了评估。为了完整起见，我们还包含了仅在s​r→o任务上训练的LSTM模型（LSTM - s）的结果。

items.en: We include the following ablations of our full model. First, we evaluate how pre-training on a large-scale corpus Radford et al. (2018) helps performance by training a comparison model from scratch, denoted ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 (- pretrain) in Table 6. Second, in our main model, we map relation names to natural language (e.g., IsA →→\to “is a”; HasSubevent →→\to “has subevent”) so the model can learn to represent these concepts with language, as opposed to learning a special embedding from scratch for each relation Levy et al. (2017). As an ablation, we train a model without converting relation tokens to natural language (e.g., IsA ↛↛\not\to “is a”), which we denote ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 - RelTok.
items.zh: 我们包含了对完整模型的以下消融实验。首先，我们通过从头训练一个比较模型（在表6中记为ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 (- pretrain)）来评估在大规模语料库上进行预训练（Radford等人，2018）对性能的帮助。其次，在我们的主要模型中，我们将关系名称映射为自然语言（例如，IsA → “is a”；HasSubevent → “has subevent”），以便模型能够学习用语言表示这些概念，而不是为每个关系从头学习一个特殊的嵌入（Levy等人，2017）。作为消融，我们训练了一个不将关系标记转换为自然语言的模型（例如，IsA ↛ “is a”），记为ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 - RelTok。

items.en: Our results indicate that high-quality knowledge can be generated by the model: the low perplexity scores in Table 6 indicate high model confidence in its predictions, while the high classifier score (95.25%) indicates that the KB completion model of Li et al. (2016) scores the generated tuples as correct in most of the cases. While adversarial generations could be responsible for this high score, a human evaluation (following the same design as for Atomic) scores 91.7% of greedily decoded tuples as correct. Randomly selected examples provided in Table 7 also point to the quality of knowledge produced by the model.
items.zh: 我们的结果表明，模型可以生成高质量的知识：表6中的低困惑度分数表明模型对其预测具有高置信度，而高分类器分数（95.25%）表明Li等人（2016）的KB补全模型在大多数情况下将生成的元组评分为正确。虽然对抗性生成可能导致这个高分，但人工评估（遵循与Atomic相同的设计）将91.7%的贪心解码元组评分为正确。表7中提供的随机选择示例也表明了模型生成的知识的质量。

items.en: In addition to being high quality, the generated tuples from ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 are also novel, with 59.25% of the tuples not being present in the training set, showing that the model is capable of generating new edges between nodes, and even creating new nodes – 3.75% of o𝑜o nodes are novel – to extend the size of the knowledge graph. One shortcoming, however, is that novel generations are sometimes simplified forms of tuples from the training set. In Table 7, for example, the tuple “doctor CapableOf save life” is not present in the training set, but “doctor CapableOf save person life” is. Many tuples, however, are completely novel, such as “bird bone HasProperty fragile” and “driftwood AtLocation beach”, which have no related tuples in the training set.
items.zh: 除了高质量之外，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋生成的元组也是新颖的，其中59.25%的元组不存在于训练集中，表明模型能够生成节点之间的新边，甚至创建新节点——3.75%的o节点是新颖的——从而扩展知识图的规模。然而，一个缺点是，新颖的生成有时是训练集中元组的简化形式。例如，在表7中，元组“doctor CapableOf save life”不存在于训练集中，但“doctor CapableOf save person life”存在。然而，许多元组是完全新颖的，例如“bird bone HasProperty fragile”和“driftwood AtLocation beach”，它们在训练集中没有相关元组。

items.en: To explore further, we investigate by how much novel tuples from the development set differ from training set phrase objects for the same s,r𝑠𝑟s,r using minimum edit distance of phrase objects.
items.zh: 为了进一步探索，我们研究了开发集中的新颖元组与相同s,r的训练集短语对象之间的最小编辑距离。

items.capEn: Figure 4: The percentage of novel ConceptNet development set tuples per minimum edit distance from training tuples. In green: classifier-scored accuracy of each subset.
items.capZh: 图4：每个最小编辑距离下新颖的ConceptNet开发集元组占训练元组的百分比。绿色：每个子集的分类器评分准确率。

items.en: We measure the edit distance of phrase object od​e​vsubscript𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑣o_{dev} in the tuple (s,r,od​e​v)𝑠𝑟subscript𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑣(s,r,o_{dev}) to the ot​r​nsubscript𝑜𝑡𝑟𝑛o_{trn} from the nearest training tuple (s,r,ot​r​n)𝑠𝑟subscript𝑜𝑡𝑟𝑛(s,r,o_{trn}). Edit distance is measured using word tokens (excluding stop words) and normalized by the maximum number of words in od​e​vsubscript𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑣o_{dev} or ot​r​nsubscript𝑜𝑡𝑟𝑛o_{trn}. The maximum edit distance is one (i.e., entirely different word sequences) and the minimum edit distance is zero (i.e., the same sequence excluding stopwords). Figure 4 shows the percentage of novel development set tuples that have an edit distance from the closest training set tuple of at least the value on the x-axis. Over 75% of the novel tuples have objects that are a normalized edit distance of >=0.5absent0.5>=0.5 from the training phrase objects, indicating that most of the novel phrase objects have significantly different word sequences from their closest analogues in the training set.
items.zh: 我们测量元组(s,r,o_dev)中的短语对象o_dev与最近训练元组(s,r,o_trn)中的o_trn之间的编辑距离。编辑距离使用词标记（排除停用词）测量，并按o_dev或o_trn中的最大词数进行归一化。最大编辑距离为1（即完全不同的词序列），最小编辑距离为0（即排除停用词后的相同序列）。图4显示了新颖的开发集元组中，与最近训练集元组的编辑距离至少为x轴值的百分比。超过75%的新颖元组的对象与训练短语对象的归一化编辑距离≥0.5，表明大多数新颖短语对象与训练集中最接近的类比对象具有显著不同的词序列。

items.en: Similarly to Atomic, we explore how pre-training ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 on a large language corpus affects its ability to generalize commonsense. This effect is apparent in Table 6, with a clear improvement on automatic and human evaluations by the pretrained ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 over the randomly initialized model. Qualitatively, we observe this effect in Table 7 with the generated example tuple “mango IsA fruit", which is not present in the training set. The only tuple containing the “mango" entity in the training set is “mango UsedFor salsa", which is not informative enough. As confirmation, we observe that the output from ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 (- pretrain) is “mango IsA spice”, which could be a reasonable inference given the information about “mango" in the seed set of knowledge.
items.zh: 与Atomic类似，我们探索了在大语言语料库上预训练ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋如何影响其泛化常识的能力。这种效果在表6中很明显，预训练的ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋在自动和人工评估上比随机初始化的模型有显著改进。定性上，我们在表7中观察到这种效果，生成的示例元组“mango IsA fruit”不存在于训练集中。训练集中唯一包含“mango”实体的元组是“mango UsedFor salsa”，这信息量不足。作为确认，我们观察到ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 (- pretrain)的输出是“mango IsA spice”，考虑到知识种子集中关于“mango”的信息，这可能是一个合理的推断。

items.en: While the automatic metrics point to insignificant differences when comparing models with symbol relations and those with natural language relations (Table 6), examples can provide qualitative insights into the benefits of representing relations as language. While the only non-ornithological reference to a “dove" in the ConceptNet training set is “dove CapableOf fly”, our model learns to generalize to produce the tuple “dove SymbolOf purity”. The model that uses symbol relation embeddings only manages to produce the relation “dove SymbolOf submarine”, which seems to relate “submarine" to a more nautical (and unrelated) word sense of “dove".
items.zh: 虽然自动指标表明，在比较使用符号关系的模型和使用自然语言关系的模型时差异不显著（表6），但示例可以定性说明将关系表示为语言的好处。虽然ConceptNet训练集中唯一非鸟类学参考的“dove”是“dove CapableOf fly”，但我们的模型学会了泛化，生成元组“dove SymbolOf purity”。使用符号关系嵌入的模型只能生成关系“dove SymbolOf submarine”，这似乎将“submarine”与“dove”的更航海（且不相关）的词义联系起来。

items.en: Previous work has looked at constructing knowledge bases as relational schemas using expert knowledge Lenat (1995); Bodenreider (2004); Miller (1995), semi-structured text extraction Suchanek et al. (2007); Hoffart et al. (2013); Auer et al. (2007); Bollacker et al. (2008) and unstructured text extraction Dong et al. (2014); Carlson et al. (2010); Nakashole et al. (2011, 2012); Niu (2012). In our work, we focus on construction of commonsense knowledge bases which require the use of open-text events rather than a well-defined relational schema structure. Other work in information extraction can also be applied to knowledge base construction with open-text entities Soderland et al. (2010); Etzioni et al. (2011); Fader et al. (2011); Mausam et al. (2012); Fan et al. (2010); Cui et al. (2018), but these methods typically extract explicitly stated text relations. Conversely, our approach generates new knowledge that is often unstated in text, as commonsense information typically is Gordon and Van Durme (2013).
items.zh: 先前的工作研究了使用专家知识（Lenat，1995；Bodenreider，2004；Miller，1995）、半结构化文本提取（Suchanek等人，2007；Hoffart等人，2013；Auer等人，2007；Bollacker等人，2008）和非结构化文本提取（Dong等人，2014；Carlson等人，2010；Nakashole等人，2011，2012；Niu，2012）构建关系模式的知识库。在我们的工作中，我们专注于构建常识知识库，这需要使用开放文本事件而不是定义良好的关系模式结构。信息提取的其他工作也可以应用于具有开放文本实体的知识库构建（Soderland等人，2010；Etzioni等人，2011；Fader等人，2011；Mausam等人，2012；Fan等人，2010；Cui等人，2018），但这些方法通常提取明确陈述的文本关系。相反，我们的方法生成新的知识，这些知识通常在文本中未被陈述，正如常识信息通常那样（Gordon和Van Durme，2013）。

items.en: Existing work on generation of novel commonsense knowledge has also used ConceptNet and Atomic as underlying KBs. Specifically, Li et al. (2016) proposed a set of neural network models for scoring tuples in ConceptNet. Our work differs from this approach as their models evaluate full tuples rather than learning to generate the phrases to make new nodes in the knowledge graph. Saito et al. (2018) builds upon this work by proposing a joint model for completion and generation of commonsense tuples. Their work, however, focuses on using tuple generation to augment their KB completion model, rather than to increase coverage in commonsense KB construction. Finally, Sap et al. (2019) use LSTM encoder-decoder models to generate commonsense knowledge about social situations. We use transformers and investigate the effect of using pre-trained language representations Radford et al. (2018) to initialize them.
items.zh: 关于生成新颖常识知识的现有工作也使用了ConceptNet和Atomic作为底层知识库。具体来说，Li等人（2016）提出了一组神经网络模型用于评分ConceptNet中的元组。我们的工作与这种方法不同，因为他们的模型评估完整元组，而不是学习生成短语以在知识图中创建新节点。Saito等人（2018）在此基础上提出了一种用于补全和生成常识元组的联合模型。然而，他们的工作侧重于使用元组生成来增强其KB补全模型，而不是增加常识KB构建的覆盖范围。最后，Sap等人（2019）使用LSTM编码器-解码器模型生成关于社会情境的常识知识。我们使用Transformer，并研究使用预训练语言表示（Radford等人，2018）初始化它们的效果。

items.en: Finally, our work builds on previous work on adapting pre-trained language models for various sequence labeling, classification, and NLI end tasks Radford et al. (2018); Peters et al. (2018); Devlin et al. (2018). Our research investigates how pre-trained language models can be used for large-scale commonsense KB construction by generating new graph nodes and edges between nodes.
items.zh: 最后，我们的工作建立在先前关于将预训练语言模型适应于各种序列标注、分类和NLI最终任务的工作之上（Radford等人，2018；Peters等人，2018；Devlin等人，2018）。我们的研究调查了如何通过生成新的图节点和节点之间的边，将预训练语言模型用于大规模常识知识库构建。

items.en: We introduce COMmonsense Transformers (ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋) for automatic construction of commonsense knowledge bases. ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 is a framework for adapting the weights of language models to learn to produce novel and diverse commonsense knowledge tuples. Empirical results on two commonsense knowledge bases, Atomic and ConceptNet, show that ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 frequently produces novel commonsense knowledge that human evaluators deem to be correct. These positive results point to future work in extending the approach to a variety of other types of knowledge bases, as well as investigating whether ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋 can learn to produce OpenIE-style knowledge tuples for arbitrary knowledge seeds.
items.zh: 我们引入了用于自动构建常识知识库的COMmonsense Transformers（ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋）。ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋是一个框架，用于调整语言模型的权重，以学习生成新颖且多样化的常识知识元组。在两个常识知识库Atomic和ConceptNet上的实证结果表明，ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋经常生成人类评估者认为正确的新颖常识知识。这些积极的结果指向了未来的工作，即将该方法扩展到各种其他类型的知识库，以及研究ℂ𝕆𝕄𝔼𝕋是否能够学习为任意知识种子生成OpenIE风格的知识元组。

items.en: We thank Thomas Wolf, Ari Holtzman, Chandra Bhagavatula, Peter Clark, Rob Dalton, Ronan Le Bras, Rowan Zellers and Scott Yih for helpful discussions over the course of this project, as well as the anonymous reviewers for their insightful comments. This research was supported in part by NSF (IIS-1524371, IIS-1714566, NRI-1525251), DARPA under the CwC program through the ARO (W911NF-15-1-0543), and Samsung Research. This material is based, in part, upon work supported by the National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program under Grant No. DGE-1256082.
items.zh: 我们感谢Thomas Wolf、Ari Holtzman、Chandra Bhagavatula、Peter Clark、Rob Dalton、Ronan Le Bras、Rowan Zellers和Scott Yih在本项目过程中的有益讨论，以及匿名审稿人的深刻评论。本研究部分得到了美国国家科学基金会（IIS-1524371、IIS-1714566、NRI-1525251）、DARPA通过ARO的CwC项目（W911NF-15-1-0543）和三星研究的支持。本文基于的工作部分得到了美国国家科学基金会研究生研究奖学金计划的支持，资助号为DGE-1256082。
